name: ServiceNow, Inc.

profile.description: ServiceNow is the leading enterprise AI platform, orchestrating 80 billion+ enterprise workflows annually across IT, HR, customer service, security, and finance. The company's Now Platform connects systems, data, and processes with AI agents to eliminate friction in enterprise operations. ServiceNow serves over 8,100 customers including 85% of the Fortune 500. Q1 2026 subscription revenues reached $3,671M (+22% YoY), and the Board defines the company's strategic vision as becoming the 'Defining AI Enterprise Software Company of the 21st Century.'

innovation.label: STRONG
innovation.analysis: ServiceNow has fundamentally transformed from an ITSM ticketing system to an enterprise AI platform — a business model pivot that expanded TAM from ~$20B to $220B+. Innovation track record: 3pts (ITSM to enterprise platform to AI enterprise platform — two successful transformations in 20 years)[1]. AI deployment: 2pts — AI agents are shipping to customers with measurable adoption: Q4 2025 had 244 transactions over $1M in net new ACV (nearly 40% YoY growth), Q1 2026 subscription revenues grew 22% to $3,671M, and the GenAI 'Pro Plus' tier is actively pricing AI capabilities[1][2]. Market share: 2pts — category leader in enterprise workflow orchestration with no close independent competitor; 85% Fortune 500 penetration[1]. Pipeline: 2pts — Armis cybersecurity acquisition expected to close, Moveworks AI integration contributing ~1% revenue, agentic AI capabilities named and shipping[2]. R&D: 0pts — at sector average for enterprise software. Total: 9.

inspiration.label: STRONG
inspiration.analysis: ServiceNow's mission — 'to make the world of work, work better for people' — is specific, thoughtful, and connects the platform to genuine societal benefit by removing friction from the workplace to free humans for higher-value work[2]. Mission floor: 3pts (specific and validated by product outcomes — 80B+ workflows orchestrated annually). Community impact: 1pt — ServiceNow.org provides platform access to nonprofits, and the company achieved carbon neutrality with science-based targets approved by SBTi[3]. Employee retention: 1pt — McDermott's 'people before profits' and 'culture is the ultimate competitive advantage' philosophy, combined with CEO approval ratings above 90%, suggests above-average retention for enterprise software[2]. Insider buying: 2pts — CEO Bill McDermott purchased $3M of NOW shares personally during the January 2026 software sector rout when the stock dropped 10%+ on AI disruption fears, and extended his contract to 2030[2]. Total: 3 + 1 + 1 + 2 = 7.

infrastructure.label: STRONG
infrastructure.analysis: ServiceNow's financial infrastructure is near-exemplary: Q1 2026 subscription revenues $3,671M (+22% YoY), FY2026 guidance $15.53-15.57B (~20% growth), cRPO $12.85B (+25% YoY), non-GAAP operating margin 31-32%[1]. Post-5:1 split at $103.30, the stock trades at ~20.6x forward earnings. D/E 20.7%: 1.0. ROE 16.1%: 1.0 (2x weight). Revenue growth YoY 22.1%: 1.0. Revenue growth 5yr CAGR ~23% (from ~$5.9B 2021 to $14B 2025): 1.0 (2x weight). Forward P/E 20.6: (60-20.6)/(60-20)=0.986. Profit margin 12.6%: (12.6/20)=0.629. PEG 1.0: (3-1)/(3-1)=1.0 (2x weight)[4]. Weighted sum: (1x1.0)+(2x1.0)+(1x1.0)+(2x1.0)+(1x0.986)+(1x0.629)+(2x1.0) = 9.616/10 = infrastructure 10.

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: ServiceNow's core thesis is that AI creates more demand for enterprise workflow orchestration, not less. CEO McDermott has articulated persuasively: 'AI excels at identifying problems and providing advice but cannot execute solutions' — and ServiceNow controls the execution layer[2]. The Board defines NOW as the 'Defining AI Enterprise Software Company of the 21st Century,' and the company is aggressively pricing GenAI capabilities through its Pro Plus tier. JPMorgan flagged ServiceNow as an 'AI-resilient' software name, citing its role as connective tissue integrating AI insights into reliable, auditable enterprise actions[2]. The company orchestrates 80B+ workflows annually — AI agents need orchestration infrastructure to function, making ServiceNow infrastructure for AI rather than a target of AI disruption. Risk protection: 8 (IMMUNE — enterprise workflow execution layer that AI requires, not competes with; AI data centers and agents increase demand for orchestration). AI opportunity: 1 (Moveworks AI customer service generating quantifiable revenue contribution, GenAI Pro Plus tier actively priced and sold). Total: 9.

infinite_game.summary: Bill McDermott operates explicitly with long-term platform thinking. His Rule of 55+ profitability standard (revenue growth + FCF margin > 55%), contract extension to 2030, $3M personal share purchase during the January 2026 software rout, and 'culture is the ultimate competitive advantage' philosophy all reflect genuine infinite game commitment. ServiceNow's Just Cause of removing workplace friction is validated by 22% subscription revenue growth sustained for multiple years and NRR above 125%.

just_cause.analysis: ServiceNow's cause — making work work better for people by eliminating enterprise friction — is genuine, service-oriented, and specific[2]. It describes a future state (frictionless enterprise workflows) that doesn't fully exist yet and invites technology partners and customers to contribute. McDermott's consistent articulation across economic cycles and competitive environments, combined with 85% Fortune 500 penetration, validates the cause through execution. Score: 2 points (genuine cause, partially validated), display 7.

trusting_teams.analysis: McDermott's 'people before profits' philosophy and stated belief that 'culture is the ultimate competitive advantage' create organizational alignment[2]. CEO approval ratings above 90%, investment in employee AI training, and the 'human-AI' narrative — framing AI as amplifying employees rather than replacing them — create psychological safety. The company tied executive compensation to ESG targets including human capital metrics[3]. Score: 2 points (high-trust organization), display 10.

worthy_rivals.analysis: McDermott publicly acknowledges Salesforce, SAP, and Microsoft as worthy rivals whose platform integrations push ServiceNow to develop better connectors and expand capabilities. The Moveworks and Armis acquisitions reflect competitive awareness — rivals' strengths revealing ServiceNow's portfolio gaps in AI customer service and security[2]. Score: 1 point, display 10.

existential_flexibility.analysis: The pivot from ITSM ticketing (2004-2015) to enterprise platform (2015-2020) to enterprise AI platform (2020-present) represents textbook existential flexibility — each transformation built on the previous while expanding TAM 10x[2]. The company didn't defend its ITSM monopoly; it redefined the category. Now positioning as AI infrastructure for enterprise is a third transformation. Score: 2 points (transformative flexibility), display 10.

courage_to_lead.analysis: McDermott's $3M personal share purchase during the January 2026 software sector rout — when the stock dropped 10%+ on AI disruption fears — is precisely Sinek's definition of courage: acting in service of the Just Cause at personal financial cost[2]. The Rule of 55+ standard maintains long-term capital discipline. However, a true pattern of courage (2 points) requires multiple examples of sacrificing near-term results. Score: 1 point (some courageous decisions), display 6.

investment_thesis: ServiceNow is the enterprise AI platform that benefits from — rather than is threatened by — the AI boom. Q1 2026 subscription revenues grew 22% to $3,671M, cRPO of $12.85B grew 25% YoY, and FY2026 revenue guidance of $15.53-15.57B sustains ~20% growth at $14B+ scale[1]. The post-split valuation of ~20.6x forward earnings (PEG 1.0) is reasonable for a company delivering 22% revenue growth with 31-32% operating margins. CEO McDermott's $3M share purchase, 2030 contract extension, and the Board's vision of NOW as the 'Defining AI Enterprise Software Company of the 21st Century' signal maximum conviction[2]. The Armis cybersecurity acquisition adds security TAM, and GenAI Pro Plus pricing converts the AI wave into premium revenue.
thesis_continued: The bear case is that agentic AI could mature faster than McDermott's 'execution layer' thesis anticipates — if AI systems can both identify problems AND execute solutions without ServiceNow's orchestration layer, the platform becomes redundant within 3-5 years. At 61x trailing earnings (20.6x forward), any execution stumble would compress the multiple severely. Operating expenses grew 76% in recent periods for peers like Circle, and ServiceNow's own profit margin of 12.6% is modest relative to its $107B market cap[1]. The rapid pace of AI evolution creates genuine uncertainty about whether enterprise workflow platforms add value or add friction in an AI-native enterprise stack. Additionally, the 5:1 stock split in 2025 has attracted retail trading that may amplify volatility.
key_catalysts: Armis cybersecurity acquisition closing in H2 2026, adding ~1% incremental revenue and expanding platform TAM into security operations orchestration
GenAI Pro Plus tier adoption metrics materializing in Q2-Q3 2026 results with specific AI workflow automation data and pricing uplift cited
Rule of 55+ standard maintained through FY2026, validating that AI opportunity is additive to — not competitive with — platform subscription revenues
key_risks: Agentic AI systems maturing to replace enterprise workflow orchestration platforms within 3-5 years, faster than the 'execution layer' thesis anticipates
Premium valuation (~20.6x forward, 61x trailing) leaving minimal margin of safety if any single quarter misses guidance or subscription growth decelerates below 20%
Armis integration complexity and Moveworks post-acquisition execution risk creating distraction during a critical AI platform transition period
sources: ServiceNow Q4 2025/FY2025 and Q1 2026 Financial Results, SEC Form 8-K, January/April 2026
ServiceNow CEO Bill McDermott Leadership and AI Vision, BBN Times/FinancialContent/CNBC, 2025-2026
ServiceNow ESG Initiatives — Carbon Neutrality, SBTi Targets, Executive Compensation, Proxy Statement 2026
